summary: This task was mostly benchmark-runner plumbing, not new compiler/runtime work. The narrowest useful slice was to treat the existing interop families as one suite-level scorecard and verify both summarize output and series alias expansion.
ergonomics: `benchmarks/run-benchmark.mjs` already had a clean suite-comparison shape from `main-public-app-comparison`, so adding one more benchmark suite was straightforward once that path was generalized.
`benchmarks/test-series-summary.sh` is effective but getting long; adding one more suite means duplicating a fair amount of synthetic result setup and grep assertions.
Full verification is expensive because benchmark prep for Clasp tasks regenerates compiler artifacts repeatedly, so small benchmark-runner changes still pay a noticeable end-to-end cost.
follow_ups: Factor the suite-comparison printing in `benchmarks/run-benchmark.mjs` a bit further so future benchmark suites only need data declarations, not more summary formatting duplication.
Add a dedicated mixed-stack series assertion for `benchmarks/run-claude-series.sh` if this suite is expected to be part of public cross-harness reporting.
Consider a small helper in `benchmarks/test-series-summary.sh` for writing mirrored synthetic result batches by series name to cut down repetitive fixture data.
warnings: The mixed-stack suite comparison groups by shared series prefix and benchmark mode, so synthetic or real result notes must line up across all eight task runs or the suite section will not appear.
`benchmarks/test-series-summary.sh` also exercises real `prepare` paths for some Clasp workspaces; failures there can look like summary regressions even when the alias/comparison logic is correct.
The benchmark wrappers advertise aliases in hardcoded usage strings; when adding a new alias, update both wrapper scripts and the `run-benchmark.mjs` usage text or the surface drifts.
residual_risks: The mixed-stack suite currently rolls up `npm`, `Python`, `Rust`, and unsafe interop-boundary tasks only; if the benchmark program later wants secrets, audit, or persistence included in the same semantic-layer scorecard, the suite definition will need another explicit expansion.

Repeated-run wrapper coverage was added for the Codex path via `benchmarks/test-series-summary.sh`; the Claude wrapper usage text was updated in parallel, but there is no dedicated Claude mixed-stack series assertion yet.
